text:
Instruction: Also list empty devices
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"lsblk -a"}

Instruction: Archive all filepattern-*2009* files/directories under data/ into 2009.tar
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find data/ -name 'filepattern-*2009*' -exec tar uf 2009.tar '{}' +"}

Instruction: Change all file permissions to 664 and all directory permissions to 775 in directory tree "htdocs"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find htdocs -type f -exec chmod 664 {} + -o -type d -exec chmod 775 {} +"}

Instruction: Change all files in the current directory tree to owner "xx"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . \\( \\! -user xx -exec chown -- xx '{}' + -false \\)"}

Instruction: Change file permissions on all regular files within a directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/directory -type f -exec chmod 644 {} +"}

Instruction: Change permissions of all directories from the current directory tree to 644
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d -exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Change permissions of all regular files from the current directory tree to 644
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} +"}

Instruction: Change the ownership of all aluno1's files in the current directory and below to aluno2
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -user aluno1 -exec chown aluno2 {}"}

Instruction: Change the permission to 0644 for all files under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 0644 {} +"}

Instruction: Change the timestamp of symbolic link "somesymlink" to current date/time
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch -h somesymlink"}

Instruction: Clear the environment and run a program
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"env -i program"}

Instruction: Compare a single file between two branches or commits
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff branch_1..branch_2 [--] path/to/file"}

Instruction: Compare different files from the current branch to other branch
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff branch:path/to/file2 path/to/file"}

Instruction: Convert a date specified as a Unix timestamp to the default format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -d @1473305798"}

Instruction: Convert a given date to the Unix timestamp format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -d \"2018-09-01 00:00\" +%s --utc"}

Instruction: Copies ""$project_dir"/iTunesArtwork", to the 'Payload/iTunesArtwork', rewriting files if necessary.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -f \"$project_dir\"/iTunesArtwork Payload/iTunesArtwork"}

Instruction: Copies '[MacVim_source_folder]/src/MacVim/mvim' to the  '/usr/local/bin', printing info message on each operation.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -v [MacVim_source_folder]/src/MacVim/mvim /usr/local/bin"}

Instruction: Copies 'src' to 'dest' preserving overwriting the existing files.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -n src dest"}

Instruction: Copies all files like 'lib*.so' to '~/usr/gtest/lib/' directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp lib*.so ~/usr/gtest/lib"}

Instruction: Copies defined file to the target folder without overwriting existing files.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -n"}

Instruction: Copy a directory recursively, in verbose mode (shows files as they are copied)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -vR path/to/source_directory path/to/target_directory"}

Instruction: Copy a file into another directory, keeping the filename
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp path/to/source_file.ext path/to/target_parent_directory"}

Instruction: Copy a file to another location
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp path/to/source_file.ext path/to/target_file.ext"}

Instruction: Copy all regular files from the current directory tree to directory `TARGET'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec cp -t TARGET {} \\+"}

Instruction: Copy file "exist" from directory "/file/that" to a new file named "file" in "/location/for/new"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp /file/that/exists /location/for/new/file"}

Instruction: Copy multiple files at once to a directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -t path/to/destination_directory path/to/file1 path/to/file2 ..."}

Instruction: Copy src_dir recursively to dest_dir, but without overwriting existing files.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -nr src_dir dest_dir"}

Instruction: Copy text files to another location, in interactive mode (prompts user before overwriting)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -i *.txt path/to/target_directory"}

Instruction: Copy the 3 specified files to /tmp/package, preserving/creating directory structure of each file as specified on command line.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp --parents src/prog.js images/icon.jpg /tmp/package"}

Instruction: Copy the entire directory tree under t1 to t2, do not create a containing t1 directory in t2.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -R t1/ t2"}

Instruction: Create a directory named 'alpha_real' in the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir alpha_real"}

Instruction: Create a subgroup
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/group_type/group_name/subgroup_name"}

Instruction: Create a symbolic link in target directory "$tmpdir" for each file under the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $PWD -type f -exec ln -st $tmpdir {} +"}

Instruction: Create all directories in the path specified by variable $tempWork
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir -p $tempWork"}

Instruction: Create an empty file 'last.check' in directory pointed by variable "log_dir", with specified timestamp.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch -m 201111301200.00 $log_dir/last.check"}

Instruction: Create an empty file (or update timestamp of file) specified by variable "correctFilePathAndName"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch \"$correctFilePathAndName\""}

Instruction: Create an empty file called "emptyfile.c"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp /dev/null emptyfile.c"}

Instruction: Create an empty index.html, or update its timestamp if it already exists.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch index.html"}

Instruction: Create directorie(s) 'some/path' as required in the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir -p ./some/path"}

Instruction: Create directory dir2
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir dir2"}

Instruction: Create intermediate directories "tmp" as required and directories real_dir1 and real_dir2
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir -p tmp/real_dir1 tmp/real_dir2"}

Instruction: Create new branch based on a specific commit
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git branch branch_name commit_hash"}

Instruction: Create new branch based on the current commit
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git branch branch_name"}

Instruction: Create specific directories
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir path/to/directory1 path/to/directory2 ..."}

Instruction: Create specific files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch path/to/file1 path/to/file2 ..."}

Instruction: Create the directory '.npm-global' in the user's home directory(~).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir ~/.npm-global"}

Instruction: Create the directory '.npm-packages' in the user's home directory($HOME)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mkdir \"${HOME}/.npm-packages\""}

Instruction: Current Time, Month Day, Year
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +\"%T, %B %d, %Y\""}

Instruction: Current time: Hour:Minute DayOfWeek Month DayOfMonth Year
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +'%H:%M %A %B %d %Y'"}

Instruction: Current year/month/date and hour:minute:second
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +'%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S'"}

Instruction: Delete all ".DS_Store" files/directories under test directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find test -name \".DS_Store\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all broken symbolic links under '/usr/ports/packages' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -L /usr/ports/packages -type l -exec rm -- {} +"}

Instruction: Delete all directories in the /myDir directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /myDir -type d -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all directories under '.cache/chromium/Default/Cache' directory tree that are at least 1 level deep and are bigger than 100 MB in size
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find .cache/chromium/Default/Cache/ -mindepth 1 -type d -size +100M -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all empty directories in minimum 1 level down the directory 'directory'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find directory -mindepth 1 -type d -empty -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all empty directories in minimum 2 levels down the root directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find root -mindepth 2 -type d -empty -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all empty files and folders in the current directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -empty -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all empty files in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -empty -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files and directories
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files and directories in the current directory and its subdirectories.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -path '*/\\*' -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files in the "${S}/bundled-libs" folder except "libbass.so"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"${S}/bundled-libs\" \\! -name 'libbass.so' -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files in the current directory tree whose names end with ~
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*~\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files in the current directory, regardless of their name.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname '*' -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files named "filename" in the current directory tree, except the one with path ./path/to/filename
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"filename\" -and -not -path \"./path/to/filename\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files named "filename" in the current directory tree, except those with paths ending in "/myfolder/filename"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"filename\" -and -not -path \"*/myfolder/filename\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files under /path/to/files that are not newer than dummyfile
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/files -type f ! -newer dummyfile -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files under and below the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -mindepth 1 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files under root whose status were changed more than 30 minutes ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find root -type -f -cmin +30 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files/directories in minimum 2 levels down the root directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find root -mindepth 2 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files/directories named 'file' under current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name file -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files/directories older than 48 hours in /path/to/files* paths
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/files* -mtime +2 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all files/directories with '.old' extension under current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \u201c*.old\u201d -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all hidden files in the directory tree given as variable $FOLDER
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $FOLDER -name \".*\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all hidden files/directories under $some_directory including $some_directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $some_directory '.*' -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files named 'IMAGE1806.jpg' under current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name 'IMAGE1806.jpg' -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files that reside in directory $OUTPUTDIR and below, and were last modified more than 7 days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $OUTPUTDIR -type f -mtime +7 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files that start with 'sess_' in their names, are at least 1 level deep and were modified more than $gc_maxlifetime minutes ago under $save_path directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -O3 \"$save_path\" -depth -mindepth 1 -name 'sess_*' -ignore_readdir_race -type f -cmin \"+$gc_maxlifetime\" -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files under $DIR directory tree that have been modified before file $a
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"$DIR\" -type f \\! -newer \"$a\" -exec rm {} +"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files with '.cache' extension that were accessed more than 30 days ago under $HOME/Library/Safari/Icons directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $HOME/Library/Safari/Icons -type f -atime +30 -name \"*.cache\" -print -delete"}

Instruction: Delete all regular files with inode number 314167125 under current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -inum 314167125 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete files in /var/tmp/stuff and below that have not been modified in over 90 days
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/tmp/stuff -mtime +90 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete files in the DIR directory tree whose names begin with "2015" and contain "album" or "picture"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find DIR \\( -name 2015\\* -a \\( -name \\*album\\* -o -name \\*picture\\* \\) \\) -delete"}

Instruction: Delete files older than 31 days
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -mtime +31 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete files under $LOCATION that match $REQUIRED_FILES in their names and were modified more than 1 day ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $LOCATION -name $REQUIRED_FILES -type f -mtime +1 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete files under $LOCATION that match $REQUIRED_FILES in their names and were modified more than 360 minutes ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $LOCATION -name $REQUIRED_FILES -type f -mmin +360 -delete"}

Instruction: Delete in the background all files in /var/tmp/stuff1 and below that have not been modified in over 90 days
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/tmp/stuff1 -mtime +90 -delete &"}

Instruction: Display a customized summary using a comma-separated list of columns
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"lsblk --output NAME,SERIAL,MODEL,TRAN,TYPE,SIZE,FSTYPE,MOUNTPOINT,..."}

Instruction: Display a long listing of all directories under '/nas' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /nas -type d -ls"}

Instruction: Display a long listing of all files/directories that are bigger than 10MB under '/var/' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/ -size +10M -ls"}

Instruction: Display all available locales and the associated metadata
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"locale --all-locales --verbose"}

Instruction: Display all files in a folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /usr/X11/man/man5 -print"}

Instruction: Display all files in the folder home which are owned by the group test.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home -group test"}

Instruction: Display all filesystems and their disk usage using 512-byte units
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"df"}

Instruction: Display all locations containing the specified executable (works only in Bash/fish/Zsh shells)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"type -a command"}

Instruction: Display all the files/directories under '/home/bozo/projects' directory tree that have been modified within the last day
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/bozo/projects -mtime -1"}

Instruction: Display an arbitrary user's ID (UID), group ID (GID) and groups to which they belong
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id username"}

Instruction: Display current system's kernel name, kernel release and version, and machine architecture
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -srvm"}

Instruction: Display current user's ID (UID), group ID (GID) and groups to which they belong
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id"}

Instruction: Display currently logged username
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"whoami"}

Instruction: Display hardware platform, ie. x86_64 even if current kernel uses 32-bit addressing.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -i"}

Instruction: Display hostname.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -n"}

Instruction: Display information about the filesystem where a specific file is located
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat --file-system path/to/file"}

Instruction: Display information in a portable way
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"df -P"}

Instruction: Display kernel name, release, and version.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -s -r -v"}

Instruction: Display kernel release name.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -r"}

Instruction: Display key-value pairs of all environment variables
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"printenv"}

Instruction: Display long listing of all the files/directories owned by the user 'me' under '/tmp' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp -user me -ls"}

Instruction: Display machine architecture, ie. x86_64
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -m"}

Instruction: Display memory in human-readable units
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"free -h"}

Instruction: Display operating system type, ie. GNU/Linux
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -o"}

Instruction: Display properties about a specific file such as size, permissions, creation and access dates among others
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat path/to/file"}

Instruction: Display properties about a specific file such as size, permissions, creation and access dates among others without labels
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat --terse path/to/file"}

Instruction: Display reports every 2 seconds for 5 times
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"vmstat 2 5"}

Instruction: Display summary of each specified file in human readable form
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"du -sh *"}

Instruction: Display system memory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"free"}

Instruction: Display the contents of "myfile" located in the current directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cat myfile"}

Instruction: Display the contents of "text"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cat text"}

Instruction: Display the current ISO week number
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +%V"}

Instruction: Display the current date and time in the format of "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ" in UTC time.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -u +\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ\""}

Instruction: Display the current date as a Unix timestamp (seconds since the Unix epoch)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +%s"}

Instruction: Display the current date format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"locale date_fmt"}

Instruction: Display the current date in UTC, using the ISO 8601 format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"}

Instruction: Display the current date using the RFC-3339 format (`YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss TZ`)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date --rfc-3339 s"}

Instruction: Display the current date using the default locale's format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +%c"}

Instruction: Display the current primary group identity
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id -gn"}

Instruction: Display the current primary group identity as a number
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id -g"}

Instruction: Display the current user identity
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id -un"}

Instruction: Display the current user identity as a number
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"id -u"}

Instruction: Display the filesystem and its disk usage containing the given file or directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"df path/to/file_or_directory"}

Instruction: Display the name of the disk file that would be executed (works only in Bash/fish/Zsh shells)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"type -p command"}

Instruction: Display the number of lines in all regular files under current directory tree and also show the total count
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec wc -l {} +"}

Instruction: Display the type of a command
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"type command"}

Instruction: Display the type of a specific command, alias/keyword/function/builtin/file (works only in Bash/fish shells)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"type -t command"}

Instruction: Display the value of a specific variable
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"printenv HOME"}

Instruction: Display the value of a variable and end with NUL instead of newline
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"printenv --null HOME"}

Instruction: Display total apparent size of a file
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"du -sb"}

Instruction: Display version
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"gcc --version"}

Instruction: Display virtual memory statistics
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"vmstat"}

Instruction: Display who is logged on and what they are doing
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"w"}

Instruction: Do not overwrite ([n]) existing files at the target
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mv --no-clobber path/to/source path/to/target"}

Instruction: Do not prompt ([f]) for confirmation before overwriting existing files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mv --force path/to/source path/to/target"}

Instruction: Don't show untracked files in the output
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status --untracked-files=no"}

Instruction: Duplicate directory tree under /mnt/usr/lib to /usr but creating symlinks to files instead of copying them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -rs /mnt/usr/lib /usr/"}

Instruction: Enable interpretation of backslash escapes (special characters)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"echo -e \"Column 1\\tColumn 2\""}

Instruction: Ensure all 5 of UEDP0{1..5}_20120821.csv files exist, creating empty files for any missing ones (updates the file's timestamps)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch -a UEDP0{1..5}_20120821.csv"}

Instruction: Exclude directory from find . command
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ! -path \"dir1\" -iname \"*.mp3\""}

Instruction: Exclude the devices specified by the comma-separated list of major device numbers
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"lsblk -e 1,7,..."}

Instruction: Expand bash array "myargs" as arguments to "mv"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"mv \"${myargs[@]}\""}

Instruction: Find & Substitute Only 2nd Occurrence of a Word Using sed s//2 in all .txt files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed 's/Linux/Linux-Unix/2' thegeekstuff.txt"}

Instruction: Find & Write Changes to a File and Print the Changes Using sed s//gpw
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -n 's/Linux/Linux-Unix/gpw output' thegeekstuff.txt"}

Instruction: Find '.git' directories in directory tree /home/madhu/release/workspace
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/madhu/release/workspace -type d -name '.git'"}

Instruction: Find '.git' directories in directory tree /path/to/files and print the pathnames of their parents
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/files -type d -name '.git' -exec dirname {} +"}

Instruction: Find *.c and *.h files under the current directory tree skipping hidden directories and files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . \\( -path '*/.*' -prune -o ! -name '.*' \\) -a -name '*.[ch]'"}

Instruction: Find *.c and *.sh files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f \\( -name \"*.c\" -o -name \"*.sh\" \\)"}

Instruction: Find *.css files in the current directory tree, skipping all directories that match pattern '*/lang/en.css'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -path '*/lang/en.css' -prune -o -name '*.css' -print"}

Instruction: Find *.html files in the /usr/src/linux directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /usr/src/linux -name \"*.html\""}

Instruction: Find *.jpg screenshots that are bigger than 500k
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/Images/Screenshots -size +500k -iname '*.jpg'"}

Instruction: Find *.o files with permissions 664 in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name *.o -perm 664 -print"}

Instruction: Find *.pl files/directories under /users/tom
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /users/tom -name \"*.pl\""}

Instruction: Find *.scm files recursively in the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name '*.scm'"}

Instruction: Find *.txt files in the current directory tree, ignoring paths ./Movies/*, ./Downloads/*, and ./Music/*
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name \"*.txt\" ! -path \"./Movies/*\" ! -path \"./Downloads/*\" ! -path \"./Music/*\""}

Instruction: Find *log files/directories within a maximum of 3 levels of directories
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -maxdepth 3 -name \"*log\""}

Instruction: Find .jpg files owned by user daniel in the current directory and its sub-directories but ignore any file beginning with "autumn".
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -user daniel -type f -name *.jpg ! -name autumn*"}

Instruction: Find .jpg files owned by user daniel in the current directory and its sub-directories.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -user daniel -type f -name *.jpg"}

Instruction: Find .rmv files in the current directory recursively
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name *.rmv"}

Instruction: Find .txt files on the system whose size is greater than 12000 bytes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -name \"*.txt\" -size +12000c"}

Instruction: Find CSS files omitting results containing "CVS"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . \\! -path \"*CVS*\" -type f -name \"*.css\""}

Instruction: Find SGID files
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -perm +2000"}

Instruction: Find a 400 permission file under /data directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /data -type f -perm 400 -print -quit"}

Instruction: Find a directory named 'project.images' case insensitively in the entire filesystem and show it in long listing format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type d -iname \"project.images\" -ls"}

Instruction: Find a directory named 'project.images' in the entire filesystem and show it in long listing format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type d -name \"project.images\" -ls"}

Instruction: Find a more recent version of httpd.conf file than /etc/apache-perl/httpd.conf in entire file system
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -name httpd.conf -newer /etc/apache-perl/httpd.conf"}

Instruction: Find a single randomly selected executable file in the /usr/bin/ directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /usr/bin/ -type f -executable -exec shuf -n 1 -z {} +"}

Instruction: Find a size of only the target directory in MB format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"du -s --block-size=M /path/to/your/directory/"}

Instruction: Find a used disk space of a target directory and files inside of it
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"du -h your_directory"}

Instruction: Find all  'custlist*' files under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name custlist\\*"}

Instruction: Find all "G*.html" files modified more than 7 days ago in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mtime +7 -name \"G*.html\""}

Instruction: Find all $tofind* files/directories under $parentdir
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $parentdir -name $tofind*"}

Instruction: Find all '*~' files under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -name '*~'"}

Instruction: Find all 'test' directories in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -type d -a -name test"}

Instruction: Find all * * regular files under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name \"* *\""}

Instruction: Find all *$VERSION* files/directories under current directory where $VERSION is a variable
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*$VERSION*\""}

Instruction: Find all *-* files under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name '*-*'"}

Instruction: Find all *.$input_file_type files/directories under $source_dir
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"$source_dir\" -name *.$input_file_type"}

Instruction: Find all *.$input_file_type files/directories under $source_dir with the null character as the delimiter

Instruction: Find all directories that have been modified in the last seven days.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mtime -7 -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories that start with stat
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d \u2013iname stat*"}

Instruction: Find all directories under $ROOT_DIR and show the sub-directories of the directories before the directories themselves
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $ROOT_DIR -type d -depth -print"}

Instruction: Find all directories under $path
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $path -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under ${1:-.} directory without descending into any sub-directories
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ${1:-.} -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under '/nas' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /nas -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under '/var/www' directory tree excluding '/var/www/web-release-data' and '/var/www/web-development-data' directories and their sub-directories
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/www -type d \\( ! -wholename \"/var/www/web-release-data/*\" ! -wholename \"/var/www/web-development-data/*\" \\)"}

Instruction: Find all directories under 'A' directory tree excluding paths containing the directory 'a'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find A -type d \\( ! -wholename \"A/a/*\" \\)"}

Instruction: Find all directories under 'project' directory without going into subdirectories that do not match the POSIX egrep regex $PATTERN in their paths and are not empty
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find project -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -regextype posix-egrep ! -iregex $PATTERN ! -empty -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under 'test' directory tree that match the regex '.*/course[0-9.]*' in their paths
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find test -type d -regex '.*/course[0-9.]*'"}

Instruction: Find all directories under 'test' directory tree that match the regex '[course*]' in their paths
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find test -regex \"[course*]\" -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under 'test' directory tree whose paths match the regex '.*/course[0-9]\.[0-9]\.[0-9]\.[0-9]$'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find test -type d -regex '.*/course[0-9]\\.[0-9]\\.[0-9]\\.[0-9]$'"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /directory-path and change their permission to 2755
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /directory-path -type d -exec sudo chmod 2775 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /fss/fin
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /fss/fin -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/me
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/me -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/me/"$d"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/me/\"$d\" -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/me/target_dir_1
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/me/target_dir_1 -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/mywebsite/public_html/sites/all/modules and set their permission to 750
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/mywebsite/public_html/sites/all/modules -type d -exec chmod 750 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/public_html/modules  and set their permission to 750
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/public_html/modules -type d -exec chmod 750 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/public_html/sites/all/modules and set their permission to 750
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/public_html/sites/all/modules -type d -exec chmod 750 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/public_html/sites/all/themes and set their permission to 750
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/public_html/sites/all/themes -type d -exec chmod 750 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/public_html/sites/default/files and set their permission to 770
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/public_html/sites/default/files -type d -exec chmod 770 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/public_html/themes and set their permission to 750
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/public_html/themes -type d -exec chmod 750 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /home/username/tmp and set their permission to 770
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/username/tmp -type d -exec chmod 770 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /path/to/base/cache, /path/to/base/tmp, /path/to/base/logs  and change their permission to 755
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/base/cache /path/to/base/tmp /path/to/base/logs -type d -exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under /path/to/dir and change their permission to 755
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/dir -type d -exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under and below /root that match pattern "*linux*", case insensitive
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /root -type d -iname \"*linux*\""}

Instruction: Find all directories under and below parent_directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find parent_directory -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory and set their permission to 755
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -type d exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory and set their permission to 775
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -type d exec chmod 775 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory excluding  directories (along with their contents) that start with a . (dot) in their names
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -type d -a ! -name '.?*' -o ! -prune"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory tree that match the case insensitive regex '^\./course\([0-9]\.\)*[0-9]$' in their paths
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d -iregex '^\\./course\\([0-9]\\.\\)*[0-9]$'"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory tree that were modified $FTIME days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d -mtime $FTIME"}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory whose names are 33 characters long
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d -name \"?????????????????????????????????\""}

Instruction: Find all directories under current directory whose paths are 5 characters long
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -regextype posix-extended -type d -regex \".{5}\""}

Instruction: Find all directories under dir whose names are 33 characters long
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find dir -name '?????????????????????????????????'"}

Instruction: Find all directories under htdocs directory and set their permission to 775
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find htdocs -type d -exec chmod 775 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all directories under minimum 1 level down the  $GIVEN_DIR directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"$GIVEN_DIR\" -type d -mindepth 1"}

Instruction: Find all directories under minimum 1 level down the  $GIVEN_DIR directory with null character as the delimiter
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"$GIVEN_DIR\" -type d -mindepth 1 -print0"}

Instruction: Find all directories under minimum 1 level down the current directory excluding  directories (along with their contents) that start with a . (dot) in their names
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mindepth 1 -name '.*' -prune -o \\( -type d -print \\)"}

Instruction: Find all directories under minimum 2 levels down the mydir directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find mydir -mindepth 2 -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under mydir
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find mydir -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under path_to_dir directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find path_to_dir -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under present working directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $PWD -type d"}

Instruction: Find all directories under ~/code without descending into hidden directories and print them appended with : (colon)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/code -name '.*' -prune -o -type d -printf ':%p'"}

Instruction: Find all directories whose name is Tecmint in / directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type d -name Tecmint"}

Instruction: Find all directories whose name is root in / directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type d -name root"}

Instruction: Find all directories whose status were changed $FTIME days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type d -ctime $FTIME"}

Instruction: Find all directories with a name ending in ".jpg" and change the permissions to 644 for all of them at once.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -type d -name \"*.jpg\" -exec chmod 644 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all empty directories in minimum 2 levels down the root directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find root -mindepth 2 -type d -empty"}

Instruction: Find all empty directories under /tmp
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp -type d -empty"}

Instruction: Find all empty files in /tmp
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp -type f -empty"}

Instruction: Find all empty regular files in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -size 0c -type f"}

Instruction: Find all executable files under the current directory and below
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -perm /a=x"}

Instruction: Find all executable symlinks or upvoter-* files under maximum 1 level down the {} directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find {} -name 'upvoter-*' -type f -or -type l -maxdepth 1 -perm +111"}

Instruction: Find all executable upvoter-* files (following symlinks) under maximum 1 level down the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -L -maxdepth 1 -name 'upvoter-*' -type f -perm /111"}

Instruction: Find all executable upvoter-* files/symlinks under maximum 1 level down the {} directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find {} -name 'upvoter-*' \\( -type f -or -type l \\) -maxdepth 1 -perm +111"}

Instruction: Find all executables in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -executable"}

Instruction: Find all executables under /path directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path -perm /ugo+x"}

Instruction: Find all fglrx-libGL* files under and below debian/fglrx/
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find debian/fglrx/ -name 'fglrx-libGL*'"}

Instruction: Find all fglrx-libglx* files under and below debian/fglrx/
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find debian/fglrx/ -name 'fglrx-libglx*'"}

Instruction: Find all file1 and file9 files/directories under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name file1 -or -name file9"}

Instruction: Find all filename.* files/directories under /root/directory/to/search
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /root/directory/to/search -name 'filename.*'"}

Instruction: Find all filenames ending with .c in the /usr directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /usr -name \"*.c\""}

Instruction: Find all filenames ending with .c in the current directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name \"*.c\""}

Instruction: Find all filenames ending with .c in the current directory tree, case insensitive
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -iname \"*.c\""}

Instruction: Find all files (-type f) and execute the command (sed -i 's/"$2"/"$3"/g') on each of them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -type f -exec sed -i 's/\"$2\"/\"$3\"/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (-type f) in the current directory (~) and execute the sed command (-exec sed) to replace all non-whitespace characters (\S) with themselves (\S) on each file ( {} +).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -type f -exec sed -i 's/\\S/\\S/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (-type f) modified within the last day (-mtime -1) and execute the command "sed -i 's/oldstring/newstring/g' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type f -mtime -1 -exec sed -i 's/oldstring/newstring/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (-type f) that have been modified in the last 10 minutes (-mmin -10) and delete them (-delete).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type f -mmin -10 -delete"}

Instruction: Find all files (-type f) that have been modified in the last 10 minutes (-mmin -10) and execute the command "sed -i 's/\t/    /g' {} +" on them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -type f -mmin -10 -exec sed -i 's/\\t/ /g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (including hidden files) in the current directory and its subdirectories, whose name matches the pattern "*.*" and which have been modified in the last 60 minutes, excluding files whose name ends with ".tar.gz".
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -iname \"*.*\" -not -iregex \".*\\.tar\\.gz$\" -mmin -60"}

Instruction: Find all files (indicated by '*') in the current directory and its subdirectories, and move them to the 'tmp' directory in the home directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name '*' -type f -exec mv -t ~/tmp {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (not directories) and execute a sed command to convert all characters to uppercase for each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . ! -type d -exec sed -i 's/\\(.*\\)/\\u\\1/i' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (not directories) in the current directory (not subdirectories) that do not have a ".txt" extension and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type f ! -name \"*.txt\" -exec rm -f {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (not directories) in the current directory and all subdirectories and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mindepth 1 -type f -delete"}

Instruction: Find all files (not including those ending in .sh) and execute a sed command to delete any lines containing the pattern, for each of the found files.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -not -name '*.sh' -exec sed -i '' -e '/pattern/d' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (regardless of case) and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname \"*\" -type f -delete"}

Instruction: Find all files (regardless of case) with any extension in the current directory and move them to the home directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname '*.*' -type f -exec mv -t $HOME {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (regardless of name) and execute the command "chmod 600" on them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name '*.*' -exec chmod 600 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (regardless of name) in the current directory and up to three levels of subdirectories, and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 3 -type f -iname '*' -exec rm {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute a command (sed -i) to replace all occurrences of "old" with "new" in each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/old/new/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute a sed command to replace all uppercase letters with lowercase letters for each file found.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/[A-Z]/\\L&/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute a sed command to replace the last part of the filename with "txt" (\1\2txt/) for each file found ({} +).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/\\(.*\\)\\(\\.\\)\\([a-zA-Z0-9]\\+\\)/\\1\\2txt/' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command "chmod 640" on each of them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 640 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command "chmod 755" on each of them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command "chmod 774" on each of them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 774 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command "chmod a-w" on each of them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod a-w '{}' \\+"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command "chmod a-x" on each of them (the {} is a placeholder for the file name) and do this for all files at once (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod a-x {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command 'sed -i 's/Windows/Linux/g' {} +' on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/Windows/Linux/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) and execute the command 'sed -i 's/\t/ /g' {} +' on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/\\t/ /g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) in the current directory and all subdirectories (mindepth 2) and execute the command "chmod 664" on them (+).
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -mindepth 2 -type f -exec chmod 664 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) in the current directory and its subdirectories (maxdepth 2) and execute the command 'grep -ril 'text-to-search' {} +' on them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -maxdepth 2 -type f -exec grep -ril 'text-to-search' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) with a name ending in .txt and execute the command sed -i 's/an/the/g' on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name '*.txt' -exec sed -i 's/an/the/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) with any name (name "*.*") and execute the command 'sed -i 's/foo/bar/' {} +' on them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name \"*.*\" -exec sed -i 's/foo/bar/' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files (type f) with permission 0777 and execute the command "ls -lah" on each of them, displaying the results together.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / - type f -perm 0777 -exec ls -lah {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files accessed on the 29th of September, 2008, starting from the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -newerat 2008-09-29 ! -newerat 2008-09-30"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories in the current directory and its subdirectories up to 4 levels deep that have permissions set to 664 and print the last modified time and the path of each file/directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /* -maxdepth 4 -perm 664 -printf '%T+ %p\\n'"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories in the current directory and its subdirectories, and change the permissions to remove all permissions for all users except the owner, and give the owner read, write, and execute permissions.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -exec chmod -go-rwx,u+rwx {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories in the current directory recursively that contain spaces in their names
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name '* *'"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories in the current directory tree except those whose name is "dirname", case insensitive
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -iname ! -iname dirname"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories on the system whose name is `filedir'
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -name filedir"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories that do not match the pattern given as the $controlchars variable
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . ! -name \"$controlchars\""}

Instruction: Find all files and directories that have been modified in the last seven days.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mtime -7"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories under current directory without crossing over to other partitions
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -xdev -print0"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories whose names end in ".rpm", ignoring removable media, such as cdrom, floppy, etc.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -xdev -name \\*.rpm"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories with permissions 664
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -perm 664"}

Instruction: Find all files and directories, and execute a case-insensitive search for the given string in each of them, printing the name of each file or directory where a match is found.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name '*' -exec grep -ilF \"*\" {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files and set their permissions to 644.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -exec chmod 644 \"{}\" +"}

Instruction: Find all files called "file1.txt" that reside under and below /home/wsuNID/
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/wsuNID/ -name file1.txt"}

Instruction: Find all files called wp-config.php in the /var/www directory and below
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/www/ -name wp-config.php"}

Instruction: Find all files changed on the 29th of September, 2008, starting from the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -newerct 2008-09-29 ! -newerct 2008-09-30"}

Instruction: Find all files ending with ".tmp" in the current directory and its subdirectories and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -name '*.tmp' -delete"}

Instruction: Find all files ending with .tmp that were modified in the last 24 hours and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -mtime 0 -type f -name '*.tmp' -exec rm -f {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files files under the current directory except *.txt
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -type f -not -regex '.*\\.txt'"}

Instruction: Find all files in /home/kos and below whose names end in ".tmp"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/kos -name *.tmp -print"}

Instruction: Find all files in /tmp whose names begin with the current user's name followed by "."
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp -maxdepth 1 -name \"$USER.*\""}

Instruction: Find all files in /var/www/html/zip/data/*/*/*/*/* that are older than 90 days
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var/www/html/zip/data/*/*/*/*/* -type f -mtime +90"}

Instruction: Find all files in and below all subdirectories of the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mindepth 2"}

Instruction: Find all files in and below the home directory that have been modified in the last 90 minutes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -mmin -90"}

Instruction: Find all files in entire file system which are larger than 20000KB
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type f -size +20000k"}

Instruction: Find all files in maximum 1 level down the current directory that were modified less than 1 day ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -maxdepth 1 -type f -mtime -1"}

Instruction: Find all files in maximum 1 level down the current directory that were modified less than 1 day ago from today
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -maxdepth 1 -type f -daystart -mtime -1"}

Instruction: Find all files in maximum 2 levels down the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 2 -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /etc/shadow directory that contain words with 7 or more lowercase letters.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"grep -rlE \"\\b[a-z]{7,}\\b\" /etc/shadow"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home directory with a .txt extension, and for each of those files, execute the command to add a # at the beginning of each line.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home -type f -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/^/#/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/ directory tree that were last accessed more than 7 days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home -atime +7"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/ directory tree that were last modified less than 7 days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home -mtime -7"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/myuser directory recursively that are older than 7 days
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/myuser -mtime +7 -print"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/user directory, and execute the touch command on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/user -type f -name \"*\" -exec touch {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/user* directories, up to a maximum depth of 2 levels, and change the permissions to user read/write/execute, group read/write/execute, and others no access, for all files found.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/user* -maxdepth 2 -type f -exec chmod u=rwGo,g=rwX,o= {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /home/you/ directory with a ".c" extension, modified within the last 14 days, and delete them permanently.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home/you/ -iname \"*.c\" -mtime -14 -exec rm -fv {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the /tmp directory and change their permissions to 755.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp -type f -exec chmod 755 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the Documents directory that contain a string of five capital letters and print the filenames.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"grep -lE [A-Z]{5} ~/Documents/*.txt"}

Instruction: Find all files in the Documents directory that were modified in the last 7 days, and display their size in human-readable format, adding up the total size at the end.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/Documents -mtime -7d -type f -exec du -hc {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the Documents directory that were modified within the last 7 days and print them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/Documents/* -mtime -7 -print"}

Instruction: Find all files in the `sourceDir' directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find sourceDir -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1"}

Instruction: Find all files in the `sourceDir' directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find sourceDir -mindepth 1"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (and its subdirectories) and change their permissions to 0600 (read and write for the owner, no access for anyone else) using the chmod command.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname \"*\" -exec chmod 0600 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (and its subdirectories) and rename them by replacing all non-word characters (e.g. punctuation, spaces, etc.) with underscores.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -iname '*' -exec rename 's/[^\\w]/_/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (and its subdirectories) whose name matches the pattern '*', and execute the command 'sed -i 's/ $//g' {} +' on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname '*' -exec sed -i 's/ $//g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (denoted by ~/*) with a ".txt" extension and execute the command "sed -i 's/[0-9]\{2\}/&-/g' {} +" on each of them. The command "sed -i 's/[0-9]\{2\}/&-/g' {} +" will replace any two consecutive digits with the same two digits followed by a hyphen.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/* -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/[0-9]\\{2\\}/&-/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (denoted by ~/*) with a maximum depth of 0 and a name ending in ".txt", and execute the command "sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~/* -maxdepth 0 -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files in the current directory (denoted by ~/*) with the extension .c and execute the command sed -i 's/^[ 	\t]*//' on each of them.

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name '[error,access,error_log,access_log,mod_jk.log]*.[0-9]*' -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under current directory matching the posix-egrep type regex '^.*/[a-z][^/]*$'  in their names
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -regextype posix-egrep -regex '^.*/[a-z][^/]*$' -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under current directory that were modified in the last 24 hours and also include the files that were modified in less than 1 day ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -daystart -mtime +0"}

Instruction: Find all files under current directory that were modified more than 1 day ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -mtime +1"}

Instruction: Find all files under current directory without descending into .snapshot directory that were modified in last 24 hours with null character as the delimiter
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name .snapshot -prune -o \\( -type f -mtime 0 -print0 \\)"}

Instruction: Find all files under directory tree /path/to/dir whose permissions are not 644
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/dir ! -perm 0644"}

Instruction: Find all files under images directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find images -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under maximum 1 level down the ./subfolder and ./subfolder/*/  paths
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./subfolder ./subfolder/*/ -maxdepth 1 -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under minimum 1 level down the current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mindepth 1 -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under path_to_dir
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find path_to_dir -type f"}

Instruction: Find all files under the current directory that are not the same file as "/home/nez/file.txt"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -not -samefile /home/nez/file.txt"}

Instruction: Find all files under the current directory whose filenames are not "file.txt", ignoring the case
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -not -iname file.txt"}

Instruction: Find all files under the current directory whose pathnames do not end with "Video", ignoring the case
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -not -iwholename '*Video'"}

Instruction: Find all files which  begin with 'a' or 'b' from current directory downwards and print them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name [ab]* -print"}

Instruction: Find all files whose names do not begin with "zsh" on ext3 file systems
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -fstype ext3 -name zsh*"}

Instruction: Find all files whose names end with "~" in the /home/peter directory tree, following symlinks, and delete them
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -L /home/peter -name *~ -exec rm '{}' +"}

Instruction: Find all files whose permission are 777
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type f -perm 777"}

Instruction: Find all files with '.conf' extension under '/etc' directory tree that have been modified in the last 30 minutes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /etc -name \"*.conf\" -mmin -30"}

Instruction: Find all files with '.db' extension (case insensitive) that belong to user 'exampleuser' and were modified exactly 7 days ago under '/home' directory tree

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension in the current directory and its subdirectories, and replace all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension in the current directory and its subdirectories, and replace all occurrences of "string" with "replacement" in each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -iname '*.txt' -exec sed -i 's/string/replacement/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension in the current directory and its subdirectories, and replace the word "text" with "blah" in each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -type f -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/text/blah/' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension in the entire file system and execute the command "sed -i 's/\w\+/whoa/g' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -name '*.txt' -exec sed -i 's/\\w\\+/whoa/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension, and execute the command "sed -i 's/\s$//' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name '*.txt' -exec sed -i 's/\\s$//' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a ".txt" extension, sort them randomly by the fourth column, and execute the command on all of them at once.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*.txt\" -exec sort -R -k 4 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a name containing a number, that have been accessed in the last 13 minutes, with executable permissions, in the current directory and its subdirectories, and list them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -maxdepth 2 -perm /a+x -name '*[0-9]*' -mmin 13 -exec ls {} \\+"}

Instruction: Find all files with a name ending in ".tmp" and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name '$*.tmp' -delete"}

Instruction: Find all files with a name ending in "~" that are of type "file" and execute the command "shred -n 50" on them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name \"*~\" -type f -exec shred -n 50 {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with a size of 0 bytes and delete them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ~ -size 0 -delete"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /home -type f -name '*.aac'"}

Instruction: Find all files with space in their names under current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -name '* *'"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".conf" and execute a command to replace all lines starting with "Abc" with "Off" in each of those files.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".conf" in the "/etc" directory and execute the command "sed -i 's/192.168.1.1/255.255.255.0/g' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /etc -name '*.conf' -exec sed -i 's/192.168.1.1/255.255.255.0/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".conf" in the current directory and its subdirectories up to a maximum depth of 3, owned by the user "root", and print the size of each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -maxdepth 3 -name *.conf -user root -printf \"%s\\n\""}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".gif" in the current directory and its subdirectories, and execute the command "chmod -x" on each of them.

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*.jar\" -exec sed --in-place 's/: 9$/*: */g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".jpg" in the current directory and up to three levels of subdirectories, and change the permissions to 755 for all of them.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".jpg" in the home directory and its subdirectories up to a maximum depth of 3, and move them to the Pictures directory in the home directory.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"$HOME\" -maxdepth 3 -type f -name \"*.jpg\" -exec mv -t ~/Pictures {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".log" in the "/var" directory and compress them using gzip.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".log" in the entire file system and delete them.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".log" that have been modified within the last 20 minutes, up to a maximum depth of 3 directories.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".php" in the current directory and its subdirectories, and replace all occurrences of "glob" with "globul" in each file.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -iname \"*.php\" -exec sed -i 's/glob/globul/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".png" in the entire system and move them to the folder "/pictures/".
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type f -iname '*.png' -exec mv -t /pictures/ {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".py" and execute the command "rm -f" on them.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".pyc" and delete them.

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".txt" and execute the command "sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' {} +" on each of them.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*.txt\" -exec sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' {} +"}

Instruction: Find all files with the extension ".txt" and execute the command "sed -i 's/old/new/g' {} +" on them.

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./src -maxdepth 2 \\( -name '*.php' -o -name '*.py' \\) -exec sed -i 's/\\s*$//' {} +"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status --verbose --verbose"}

Instruction: Show a graph of all commits, tags and branches in the entire repo
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git log --oneline --decorate --all --graph"}

Instruction: Show a graph of commits in the current branch using only the first line of each commit message
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git log --oneline --graph"}

Instruction: Show all files in /etc that are owned by root have been modified within the last minute
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /etc/ -user root -mtime 1"}

Instruction: Show all files that have not been accessed in the $HOME directory for 30 days or more
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $HOME -atime +30"}

Instruction: Show all network addresses of the host
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"hostname -I"}

Instruction: Show all uncommitted changes (including staged ones)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff HEAD"}

Instruction: Show an overview of which file(s) changed in each commit
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git log --stat"}

Instruction: Show changed files which are not yet added for commit
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status"}

Instruction: Show commits between two dates (yyyy-mm-dd)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git log --before \"2017-01-29\" --after \"2017-01-17\""}

Instruction: Show current date in "%Y-%m-%d" format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date \"+%Y-%m-%d\""}

Instruction: Show current host name
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"hostname"}

Instruction: Show diff statistics, like files changed, histogram, and total line insertions/deletions
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff --stat commit"}

Instruction: Show last 'count' lines in file
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"tail --lines count path/to/file"}

Instruction: Show last 'num' lines in 'file' and refresh every 'n' seconds
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"tail --lines count --sleep-interval seconds --follow path/to/file"}

Instruction: Show logged-in users info
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"w"}

Instruction: Show logged-in users info without a header
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"w -h"}

Instruction: Show only octal file permissions
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat --format=\"%a %n\" path/to/file"}

Instruction: Show only staged (added, but not yet committed) changes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff --staged"}

Instruction: Show output in [s]hort format along with [b]ranch info
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status --short --branch"}

Instruction: Show system information: kernel name, hostname, kernel release and version, machine architecture, processor type, hardware platform, and operating system type.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"uname -a"}

Instruction: Show the FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"hostname --fqdn"}

Instruction: Show the [b]ranch and tracking info
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status --branch"}

Instruction: Show the current UTC date in '%Y-%m-%dT%k:%M:%S%z' format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%k:%M:%S%z'"}

Instruction: Show the date in default format for tomorrow + 2 days + 10 minutes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -d tomorrow+2days-10minutes"}

Instruction: Show the environment
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"env"}

Instruction: Show the epoch in default date/time format
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date -ud@0"}

Instruction: Show the explanation of find's debugging options
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -D help"}

Instruction: Show the list of directories in the /mnt/raid directory tree
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /mnt/raid -type d -print"}

Instruction: Show the list of files larger than 100 MB
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -size +100M -print"}

Instruction: Show the list of files modified less than a minute ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -mmin -1 -print"}

Instruction: Show the list of files modified more than 31 days ago
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -mtime +31 -print"}

Instruction: Show the name of the current branch
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git branch --show-current"}

Instruction: Show the network address of the host name
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"hostname -i"}

Instruction: Show the number of entries currently stashed away
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git status --show-stash"}

Instruction: Show the owner and group of a specific file
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat --format=\"%U %G\" path/to/file"}

Instruction: Show the sequence of commits starting from the current one, in reverse chronological order of the Git repository in the current working directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git log"}

Instruction: Show the size of a single directory, in human-readable units
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"du -sh path/to/directory"}

Instruction: Show the size of a specific file in bytes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"stat --format=\"%s %n\" path/to/file"}

Instruction: Show unstaged changes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git diff"}

Instruction: Show version information of the find utility
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -version"}

Instruction: Sort processes by memory consumption
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"ps --sort size"}

Instruction: Start program 'scriptname' with an empty environment.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"env - scriptname"}

Instruction: Test if a file named 'file' in the current directory is more than 1 hour old
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find file -chour +1 -exit 0 -o -exit 1"}

Instruction: This command find displays the files which are modified in the last 15 minutes. And it lists only the unhidden files. i.e hidden files that starts with a . (period) are not displayed in the find output.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -mmin -15 \\( ! -regex \".*/\\..*\" \\)"}

Instruction: This command searches the file /proc/version for any strings of capital letters.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"grep -e '[A-Z]+' /proc/version"}

Instruction: This command sets the display environment variable to :0, sets the Xauthority environment variable to the user's home directory, and then runs the xset command to query the current settings of the X server.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"env DISPLAY=:0 XAUTHORITY=~/.Xauthority xset q"}

Instruction: This command will output the current date and time in the format of "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS".
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"date +\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\""}

Instruction: This find command ignore the case when searching for file name , to ignore the case  in this example all .py & .PY file will search
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -iname \"*.py\""}

Instruction: Traverse the filesystem just once, listing setuid files and directories into /root/suid.txt and large files into /root/big.txt.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / \\( -perm -4000 -fprintf /root/suid.txt '%#m %u %p\\n' \\) , \\ \\( -size +100M -fprintf /root/big.txt '%-10s %p\\n' \\)"}

Instruction: Update the timestamp of 'filename', or create an empty file if it doesn't exist.
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"touch filename"}

Instruction: Update timestamps of all regular files (ie. excluding directories, symlinks, sockets, etc.) under /your/dir
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /your/dir -type f -exec touch {} +"}

Instruction: Use 1024-byte units when writing space figures
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"df -k"}

Instruction: Use ASCII characters for tree formatting
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"lsblk -i"}

Instruction: Use the first argument as the destination directory (useful for `xargs ... | cp -t <DEST_DIR>`)
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"cp -t path/to/target_directory path/to/file_or_directory1 path/to/file_or_directory2 ..."}

Instruction: View security context of a file
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"ls -lZ path/to/file"}

Instruction: View the current IFS value
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"echo \"$IFS\""}

Instruction: View the current security context of a file or directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"ls -dlZ path/to/file_or_directory"}

Instruction: add Dockerfile to all directories in projects
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"projects\" -type d -exec cp \"Dockerfile\" \"{}/Dockerfile\" \\;"}

Instruction: add README.md to all directories in templates
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find \"templates\" -type d -exec cp \"README.md\" \"{}/README.md\" \\;"}

Instruction: delete all instances of the file "bad" if its size is 0 bytes
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name bad -empty -delete"}

Instruction: delete all text files from current folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f ! -iname \"*.txt\" -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the ".bak" or swap files in kat folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find kat -type f \\( -name \"*~\" -p -name \"*.bak\" \\) -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the backup files in current directory
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -name \"*.bak\" -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the broken symbolic links from the folder /usr/ports/packages
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -L /usr/ports/packages -type l -exec rm -- {} +"}

Instruction: delete all the empty files(files with size 0 bytes) in the current folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -empty -delete -print"}

Instruction: delete all the files ending with "~" in current folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find -name '*~' -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the files in the current folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the normal files in the current folder and do not delete those in the subfolders
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 1 -type f -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the normal/regular files in the current folder
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -type f -print -delete"}

Instruction: delete all the regular files in the temp folder which have not been modified in the last 24 hours
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /tmp/ -type f -mtime +1 -delete"}

Instruction: list all js files under currect directory exculde the directory which path contain "/path/to/search/exclude_me" or name isexclude_me_too_anywhere
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /path/to/search \\ -type d \\ \\( -path /path/to/search/exclude_me \\ -o \\ -name exclude_me_too_anywhere \\ \\) \\ -prune \\ -o \\ -type f -name '*\\.js' -print"}

Instruction: list all regular files under the directory "$directory"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find $directory -type f -name '*'"}

Instruction: list all regular files which path is not dir1 or dir2
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ! -path \"dir1\" ! -path \"dir2\" -type f"}

Instruction: list all samba files in /var/l* directory ( /var/lib or /var/log )
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /var -path */l??/samba*"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -ls -name \"*.ksh\""}

Instruction: list all the files in the file system excluding proc folder and excluding symbolic links which have write permission for the user
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -path /proc -prune -o -perm -2 ! -type l -ls"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find ./ -name \"*.sqlite\" -ls"}

Instruction: list complete path name to process associated with pid "$1"
JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find /proc/$1/exe -printf '%l\\n'"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -type d -gid 100"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find . -maxdepth 2 -type f"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"git branch"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / -mount \\! -readable -prune -o -path /dev -prune -o -name '*.jbd' -ls"}

JSON: {"mode":"shell","command":"find / \\! -readable -prune -o -name '*.jbd' -ls"}
